31.reason: Error: The loop condition should be m < i (or m <= i-1) because i is the index of the last element, not the count. This causes an out-of-bounds access when m equals i, since A[i] was never initialized (the last read was at index i-1).

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: The loop condition should be m < i (or m <= i-1) because i is the index of the last element, not the count. This causes an out-of-bounds access when m equals i, since A[i] was never initialized (the last read was at index i-1).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be m < i (not <=) because i is the index of the last element, and the loop should iterate over all elements from 0 to i-1. Using <= causes out-of-bounds access when m equals i.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be 'm < i' because 'i' is one less than the actual number of terms due to the earlier bug, but even if fixed, using '<=' will access A[i] which is uninitialized or beyond valid data.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop condition should be 'm < i' (not '<=') because 'i' is the count of terms, and indices go from 0 to i-1. Using '<=' accesses A[i], which is uninitialized or invalid.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: Same issue as above; should be n < j to avoid accessing B[j] which is uninitialized or out of bounds.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Same error: should be 'n < j', not '<=j', to avoid accessing B[j] which is out of bounds.]

45.reason: Out-of-bounds access: valid indices for C are 0 to k-1. When l equals k, C[l] is invalid. Additionally, C[l+1] is accessed inside the loop, causing out-of-bounds when l reaches k-1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: The loop condition should be l < k (or l <= k-1) because k is the count of elements, and the last valid index is k-1. When l equals k, C[l] is out of bounds.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be l < k (not <=) because k is the count of elements, and valid indices are 0 to k-1. Using <= causes out-of-bounds access when l equals k.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  loop condition l <= k leads to out-of-bounds access; valid indices are 0..k-1
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition should be l < k, and merging logic is flawed; also causes out‑of‑bounds access at l = k.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Out-of-bounds access: valid indices for C are 0 to k-1. When l equals k, C[l] is invalid. Additionally, C[l+1] is accessed inside the loop, causing out-of-bounds when l reaches k-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Loop condition should be `l < k` to prevent out-of-bounds array access when `l` reaches `k`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be 'l < k - 1' or similar; accessing C[l+1] when l == k leads to out-of-bounds read (undefined behavior). Also, this merging logic fails to combine more than two terms with same exponent.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop condition should be 'l < k - 1' or similar; using '<=k' leads to accessing C[k] and C[k+1], which are out of bounds (valid indices are 0 to k-1).]

47.reason: Accesses C[l+1] which is out-of-bounds when l is k-1. The loop condition should ensure l+1 < k, or handle the last element separately to prevent invalid memory access.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: When l equals k-1, C[l+1] accesses C[k], which is out of bounds. Also, the logic for combining like terms is incorrect; it only combines adjacent terms after sorting but does not handle multiple consecutive terms with the same exponent properly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: When l equals k-1, C[l+1] accesses C[k] which is out of bounds. Also, the logic for combining like terms is incorrect; it should combine all consecutive terms with the same exponent, not just pairs.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  accessing C[l+1] when l can be k-1 or k causes out-of-bounds; also logic fails for three or more equal exponents
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When l = k-1, C[l+1] is out of bounds; also the merging algorithm incorrectly handles consecutive same exponents.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Accesses C[l+1] which is out-of-bounds when l is k-1. The loop condition should ensure l+1 < k, or handle the last element separately to prevent invalid memory access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Accessing `C[l+1]` when `l == k-1` causes out-of-bounds read; logic fails to correctly handle the last term.

20.reason: [The increment of 'i' happens after checking for newline, so the last valid term is never counted because loop breaks before incrementing. This causes the first polynomial to miss its last term.]
